task:
more PR feedback:
idna_adapter is added as a direct dependency solely to influence transitive resolution (via url/idna, also pulled in by lettre and ureq). The inline comment frames this as only about xkcd.com, but the downgrade affects IDNA handling for the user-provided XKCD_SMTP_SERVER as well. Please either (a) document this broader behavior change explicitly (e.g., require ASCII/punycode hostnames), and/or (b) add config validation to fail fast when smtp_server contains non-ASCII so the regression is obvious.
user_feedback_text:
more PR feedback:
idna_adapter is added as a direct dependency solely to influence transitive resolution (via url/idna, also pulled in by lettre and ureq). The inline comment frames this as only about xkcd.com, but the downgrade affects IDNA handling for the user-provided XKCD_SMTP_SERVER as well. Please either (a) document this broader behavior change explicitly (e.g., require ASCII/punycode hostnames), and/or (b) add config validation to fail fast when smtp_server contains non-ASCII so the regression is obvious.

task: review new PR feedback from copilot

The new SMTP server validation tests don't cover leading/trailing whitespace or ASCII control characters (which currently pass is_ascii() and the non-empty check). Adding a test for something like " smtp.example.com " (or a newline) would lock in the intended behavior once trimming/rejecting whitespace is implemented.
user_feedback_text: The new SMTP server validation tests don't cover leading/trailing whitespace or ASCII control characters (which currently pass is_ascii() and the non-empty check). Adding a test for something like " smtp.example.com " (or a newline) would lock in the intended behavior once trimming/rejecting whitespace is implemented.

task: Co-pilot found more issues in review, despite me asking you to review this yourself. Use an adversarial review subagent if needed. Check and double-check your work before pushing your commit.

task:
thoughts on this?

Trimming XKCD_SMTP_USERNAME/XKCD_SMTP_PASSWORD changes credential semantics (it becomes impossible to use leading/trailing spaces in either value) and can also turn an all-whitespace env var into Some(""), which then passes validation and results in an attempted auth with empty credentials. Consider preserving these values verbatim, or if whitespace should be ignored, treat a post-trim empty string as unset (or surface a clear validation error).
user_feedback_text:
thoughts on this?

Trimming XKCD_SMTP_USERNAME/XKCD_SMTP_PASSWORD changes credential semantics (it becomes impossible to use leading/trailing spaces in either value) and can also turn an all-whitespace env var into Some(""), which then passes validation and results in an attempted auth with empty credentials. Consider preserving these values verbatim, or if whitespace should be ignored, treat a post-trim empty string as unset (or surface a clear validation error).
